india_trends.title: Oil Edges Lower as Trump Says US Will Help Vessels Exit Strait
india_trends.summary: Oil dropped after President Donald Trump said the US would begin guiding neutral ships out through the Strait of Hormuz and signaled progress on talks with Iran to end the war now in its 10th week.

india_trends.title: In an Age of Doom Scrolling, Asia’s Book Market Is Thriving
india_trends.summary: The internet was supposed to kill off books. But across much of Asia, a booming market tells a different story.

india_trends.title: Public sector banks rush to build up wealth management assets
india_trends.summary: The move is aimed at improving customer engagement as household savings shift from bank deposits to market-linked instruments and risk products. india_trends.title: Treasury Market on Watch for Shift in Yellen-Era Debt Playbook
india_trends.summary: Wednesday will amount to a sort of Groundhog Day for US bond dealers, who will — as has been the case for more than a year now — be watching for any change in guidance from the Treasury in its latest plan for debt issuance.

india_trends.title: Tax shadow over charitable trusts with 'profit' motive
india_trends.summary: Three top-notch hospitals in Mumbai and a famous international spiritual organisation-all functioning as charitable trusts-have been denied Income tax (I-T) exemption on the grounds that they were carrying out commercial activities.

india_trends.title: Vodafone Idea eyes ₹25,000 crore SBI-led funding after AGR relief
india_trends.summary: A State Bank of India led consortium is looking at providing ₹25,000 crore debt funding to Vodafone Idea. This comes after the government significantly reduced the telco's dues. Vodafone Idea is also seeking ₹10,000 crore for working capital. The company plans a three-year turnaround program focusin

india_trends.title: HSN classification muddle raises costs for battery storage sector
india_trends.summary: The industry has sought separate HSN codes, an approved manufacturer list and tighter local content norms, along with clarity on customs duty deferment under warehouse rules for the 12-year life of storage projects.

india_trends.title: Traders Grapple With Two-Sided Tail Risk as Stocks Regain Highs
india_trends.summary: With equities back to their highs after a V-shaped recovery from the Iran oil shock, investors are increasingly caught between left and right tail risks: The tireless AI and semiconductor rally on one side and the gradual drag from higher energy prices on the other.

india_trends.title: OPEC members increase oil production quota by 188,000 bpd, no word on UAE's withdrawal amid war in West Asia
india_trends.summary: Saudi Arabia, Russia, and five other OPEC members increased their oil production quota by 188,000 barrels per day, despite the UAE's recent withdrawal. Analysts suggest this move aims to project stability amid ongoing market disruptions from the Mideast conflict.

india_trends.title: New policy will benefit premium brands, hurt local ones, distillers tell Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah
india_trends.summary: Local alcohol manufacturers in Karnataka have urged Chief Minister Siddaramaiah to review the proposed liquor tax system, saying it favours multinational premium brands over cheaper local products.

india_trends.title: UAE oil giant ADNOC pledges $55 billion in new projects by 2028: statement
india_trends.summary: The Abu Dhabi National Oil Company on Sunday pledged to spend $55 billion on new projects over the next two years, just days after the United Arab Emirates' officially left the OPEC oil cartel. india_trends.summary: Nestle India aims for volume-led growth in the new fiscal year. The company is cautious about price volatility stemming from rising input costs and geopolitical tensions.
